software-developers: Tech companies, startups, or corporate IT teams building applications and systems. Desk-intensive, often remote; agile sprint cycles drive daily rhythm. High income; significant spending on tech hardware, SaaS tools, and online learning platforms.
solar-photovoltaic-installers: Rooftops and solar farms installing and connecting solar panel systems. Outdoor roof work; physically active; growing industry with increasing demand. Moderate income; aligns with sustainability values; invests in NABCEP certification.
special-education-teachers: Public or private schools providing individualized instruction to students with disabilities. Documentation-heavy IEP management combined with differentiated instruction. Moderate income with good benefits; invests in special education resources and accommodations.
multimedia-artists-and-animators: Film studios, game companies, or ad agencies creating animations and digital effects. Desk-based digital creative work; project-deadline driven. Good income in major studios; moderate in independent work; invests in high-end computer hardware.
speech-language-pathologists: Schools, hospitals, or private clinics diagnosing and treating communication disorders. Patient-facing clinical and educational work; documentation-heavy. High income; invests in CCC-SLP certification and AAC technology.
stationary-engineers-and-boiler-operator: Large buildings or industrial plants operating HVAC, boiler, and mechanical systems. Shift-based round-the-clock monitoring; physically active plant work. High income; union wages; invests in boiler license and refrigeration certifications.
substance-abuse-behavioral-disorder-and-: Treatment centers, hospitals, or private practice providing addiction and mental health therapy. Intensive clinical work; high burnout risk; emotionally demanding client interactions. Moderate income; invests in CADC/LCDC licensure and clinical training.
surgical-technologists: Hospital operating rooms preparing sterile fields and assisting during surgeries. Shift-based high-precision clinical work; standing for long procedures. Moderate income; invests in NCCT or CST certification.
survey-researchers: Market research firms, government agencies, or universities designing and analyzing surveys. Office-based data design and analysis work. Moderate income; invests in survey platforms (Qualtrics, SurveyMonkey) and research methods training.
surveying-and-mapping-technicians: Engineering or government agencies collecting land measurements for construction or mapping. Outdoor fieldwork with survey equipment in varied terrain. Moderate income; spends on GPS/survey equipment maintenance and GIS software.
surveyors: Land surveying firms or government agencies establishing legal boundaries and topography. Mix of outdoor fieldwork and office calculations and map production. Moderate-to-good income; invests in survey equipment and professional licensure.
tax-examiners-and-collectors-and-revenue: Government tax agencies auditing returns and collecting revenue. Desk-based with fieldwork for audits; strict rule-following environment. Stable government salary with good benefits; lower consumer spending than private-sector peers.
taxi-drivers-and-chauffeurs: Rideshare platforms or taxi companies transporting passengers. Flexible gig-style work; income tied to hours and demand. Variable income; high vehicle wear; spends on fuel and rideshare platform fees.
teacher-assistants: Schools supporting teachers with classroom management and student instruction. Part-time hours common; physically active classroom environment. Low income; often pursuing teaching credentials; limited savings and spending.
technical-writers: Tech companies or corporate departments creating manuals, guides, and technical documentation. Desk-based writing with subject matter expert interviews; remote work common. Good income; invests in writing tools and STC membership.
telecommunications-equipment-installers-: Telecom companies or contractors installing and maintaining switching and data equipment. Field-based technical work; travel to customer sites and equipment rooms. Good income; invests in network certification (BICSI) and telecom testing tools.
tellers: Bank branches processing customer deposits, withdrawals, and account transactions. Customer-facing counter role with cash handling accuracy requirements. Below-average income; often part-time entry banking role; limited spending capacity.
top-executives: C-suite or senior executive roles in corporations, nonprofits, or government. High-pressure, high-visibility leadership with board reporting and strategic decision-making. High to very high income; substantial spending on professional networks, travel, and executive lifestyle.
tour-and-travel-guides: Tourist sites, tour operators, or transportation companies leading visitor groups. Active outdoor or vehicle-based work; seasonal demand in tourist areas. Low-to-moderate income; tips supplement; values travel benefits and cultural interaction.
training-and-development-managers: Corporate L&D leadership building workforce capability at scale. Mix of strategy, program design, and stakeholder management. High income; significant investment in learning technology platforms and leadership development programs.
training-and-development-specialists: Corporate L&D departments designing and delivering employee training programs. Mix of classroom facilitation, e-learning development, and office desk work. Moderate income; heavy investment in instructional design tools and train-the-trainer courses.
transportation-storage-and-distribution-: Logistics companies or corporate supply chain departments managing warehouses and freight. Warehouse and office split; 24/7 operations often require off-hours availability. High income; spends on fleet management software and supply chain conferences.
travel-agents: Travel agencies or online booking platforms arranging trips for clients. Client-facing booking work; frequent use of GDS reservation systems. Moderate income; travel industry perks and FAM trips; values firsthand travel experience.
tutors: Students' homes, tutoring centers, or online platforms providing academic support. Flexible hourly work; income can be variable and seasonal. Variable income; often supplementary; invests in subject textbooks and online teaching tools.
umpires-referees-and-other-sports-offici: Sports leagues and events officiating games and enforcing rules. Part-time or seasonal work for most; requires physical fitness and quick judgment. Below-average income; often supplementary; invests in officiating certifications and travel.
urban-and-regional-planners: Local government or planning firms shaping land use, zoning, and development policies. Office-based with community meetings and site visits. Moderate-to-good income; invests in AICP certification and GIS software.
veterinarians: Animal hospitals, large animal practices, or research institutions providing animal healthcare. Physical patient care with significant student debt burden. High income but high debt; significant spending on veterinary equipment and CE.
veterinary-assistants-and-laboratory-ani: Animal clinics or research facilities caring for animals under veterinary supervision. Hands-on animal handling; physically active environment. Low income; animal lover motivation; spends on animal care supplies.
veterinary-technologists-and-technicians: Veterinary clinics or animal hospitals performing diagnostics and assisting with procedures. Clinical hands-on work with animals; emotionally difficult end-of-life cases. Below-average income for education level; passion-driven career choice.
waiters-and-waitresses: Casual to fine dining restaurants serving food and drinks to guests. Standing-intensive evening and weekend shifts; income heavily tip-dependent. Variable income with tips; higher in fine dining; spends on comfortable footwear and grooming.
water-and-wastewater-treatment-plant-and: Municipal water treatment facilities operating purification and distribution systems. Shift-based monitoring with equipment checks; critical public health role. Moderate-to-good income; government benefits; invests in state operator certifications.
water-transportation-occupations: Ships, ferries, or tugboats moving passengers or cargo on waterways. Extended time at sea away from home; physically active deck or engine room work. Good income with TWIC/USCG requirements; concentrated spending during shore leave.
web-developers: Digital agencies, tech companies, or freelance; building websites and web applications. Flexible work environment; often remote; mix of design and code. Moderate-to-high income; invests in design tools, hosting, and front-end framework courses.
welders-cutters-solderers-and-brazers: Manufacturing, construction, or shipyards joining metal components. Physically demanding; hot environment with fume exposure; PPE required. Moderate income; invests in welding certifications (AWS) and quality welding equipment.
wholesale-and-manufacturing-sales-repres: Manufacturers or distributors selling products to businesses and retailers. Territory-based travel to client sites; relationship management is central. Good income with commission; vehicle allowance; invests in product knowledge and sales skills.
wind-turbine-technicians: Wind farms maintaining and repairing wind turbines. Outdoor height work in remote locations; physically demanding and weather-exposed. Good income; growing renewable energy field; invests in AWEA certifications and climbing gear.
woodworkers: Furniture manufacturers or custom woodworking shops crafting wood products. Skilled craft work with power tools; artisan tradition valued. Moderate income; often small business or custom shop; invests in quality tools and lumber.
writers-and-authors: Self-employed or publishing contracts producing books, scripts, or online content. Solitary creative work; irregular income from advances and royalties. Variable income; often requires supplementary income; invests in writing software and craft courses.
zoologists-and-wildlife-biologists: Government agencies or universities studying animal behavior and ecosystems. Fieldwork in natural habitats combined with lab and office analysis. Moderate income; passion-driven career; spends on field gear and biological equipment.
civil-servant-cn: Works in government agencies at national, provincial, or local level. Job security and benefits are highly valued. Career path is predictable. Social status is high relative to salary.
food-delivery-driver: Gig economy platform worker. Daily income depends on order volume. High phone/app usage. Time pressure. Limited savings. Works through Meituan or Ele.me.
courier-express: Works for major logistics companies (SF Express, JD Logistics, Cainiao). Fixed package delivery routes. Physical labor, early hours. Income tied to parcel volume and seasonal peaks.
ride-hailing-driver: Platform worker on DiDi or similar apps. Owns or rents a car. Income is flexible but uncertain. High urban mobility. Knowledgeable about apps and navigation tech.
ecommerce-operator: Manages product listings, promotions, and customer service on Taobao/Tmall/JD. Highly data-driven. Works irregular hours during shopping festivals. Understands consumer psychology deeply.
livestream-seller: Sells products via live streaming on Douyin or Taobao Live. Income varies wildly — top performers earn millions, most earn modest income. Heavy phone usage, brand deal negotiation, fan engagement.
small-business-owner: Runs a small shop, restaurant, or service business. Self-employed, family-operated. High work hours, direct customer relationships. Income volatile and seasonal. Strong local community ties.
soe-manager: Mid-level manager at a state-owned enterprise. Strong job security and housing benefits. Bureaucratic work culture. Long tenure is typical. Social status tied to company prestige.
construction-worker-cn: Migrant worker on construction sites. Away from home for months at a time. Paid by day or project. Limited social protections. Sends remittances back to rural family.
rural-teacher: Teaches at a township or village school in China. Stable government salary but modest pay. Housing often provided. Strong community respect. Limited access to urban amenities.
public-institution-staff: Works at state-funded institutions like hospitals, universities, or research institutes. Hybrid status — not full civil servant but similar stability. Pension and healthcare benefits strong.
factory-line-worker: Assembly line worker at a manufacturing plant. Repetitive work, 8-12 hour shifts. Dormitory living common. High exposure to consumer electronics and basic goods. Price-sensitive consumer.
